titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5980

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=751 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:57:31.543227+00 | translation moa=750 status=reviewed modified=2022-10-31 20:57:31.562067+00

div.p:
Omnibus Hoc scriptum visuris vel Audituris Mariota de chinerieside quondam vxor Ricardi de
Renigtona et patricius filius suus salutem Nouerit vniuersitas vestra quod cum priorem et conuentum
de Coldingham per breue domini Regis de recognicione . Coram domino .W. Olifard tunc iusticiario
Laodonie de una carucata terre . cum pertinenciis in rennigtona traxissemus in placitum  nos omne
Ius nostrum et clamium si quod habuimus vel habere potuimus . dictis priori et conuentui et eorum successoribus
de nobis et heredibus nostris quietum clamauimus et in plena curia abiurauimus imperpetuum .
In cuius rei testimonium  presenti scripto sigilla nostra apposuimus . Hiis testibus . Domino W. de
Lindeseye Rannulfo de bonechil Domino Rad’ nobili Domino W. de morthigtona . Domino
Alano de suintona . Alano filio suo . Roberto . et Willelmo de paxtona . Ada de prenderghest
Ada filio Iohannis . de aytona . Bertramo de Ristona Iohanne filio ingrami et Aliis

p.cl: To all who shall view or hear this written document, Mariota of Chirnside, formerly the wife of Richard of Renton, and Patrick her son send greeting.

Know you all that since we have taken the prior and convent of Coldingham to court, before the lord Walter Oliphant, then justiciar of Lothian, by a brieve of recognition of the lord king, concerning one ploughgate of land with pertinents in Renton, we have quitclaimed and renounced in full court, in respect of ourselves and our heirs, all our right and claim, if we had or could have had any, to the said prior and convent and their successors.
As witness of this matter, we have put our seals to this present document.

With these witnesses: the lord W. of Lindsay, Ranulf of Bunkle, the lord Ralph Noble, the lord William of Mordington, the lord Alan of Swinton, Alan his son, Robert and William of Paxton, Adam of Prenderguest, Adam son of John of Ayton, Bertram of Reston, John son of Ingram, and others.
